PLAN/ROADMAP.md:11:Before any code touches the browser or downloader, run a brainstorm session to nail: PLAN/ROADMAP.md:13:- What does a "mod browser" actually need to do? (search, filter, preview, one-click queue?) PLAN/ROADMAP.md:14:- How much of the metadata lives in `coh-mods-list.txt` today vs. what's missing (names, descriptions, categories, download counts)? PLAN/ROADMAP.md:33:- Category filter (Maps / Audio / GUI / Popmenus / Graphics / Cursors / Languages / Other) PLAN/ROADMAP.md:34:- Sort order (downloads ↓, name A–Z, newest) PLAN/ROADMAP.md:35:- Search / keyword filter PLAN/ROADMAP.md:50:- Filter sidebar + search bar + category tabs PLAN/ROADMAP.md:51:- Each mod card shows: name, category badge, download count, description snippet, one-click PLAN/ROADMAP.md:52: download button / queue button PLAN/ROADMAP.md:62:Current state of `download-mods.py`: PLAN/ROADMAP.md:67:- No post-download verification (zip integrity check) PLAN/ROADMAP.md:78:| **Integrity** | `zipfile.is_zipfile()` check after download; log corrupt files | PLAN/ROADMAP.md:79:| **Dry run** | `--dry-run` flag to list what would be downloaded without fetching | PLAN/ROADMAP.md:80:| **Install step** | Optional `--install` flag to copy extracted assets to LaunchCat Wine prefix | PLAN/ROADMAP.md:85:Deliverable: updated `download-mods.py` (v2), changelog comment block at top of file. PLAN/ROADMAP.md:95:- Test: filter by each category, search for a known mod name, sort in each direction PLAN/ROADMAP.md:96:- Test: card hover states, download button behavior, any JS edge cases (empty results, long names) PLAN/ROADMAP.md:103:- Generate a `mods.json` from the enriched mod list (name + category + download count + URL) PLAN/ROADMAP.md:105:- `download-mods.py --from mods.json` uses it as the canonical source PLAN/ROADMAP.md:128: python-expert enhances downloader docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:23:`mods.json` embedded as inline JS (`const MODS = [...]`) at write time. docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:24:Schema per entry: `id, name, author, category, downloads, description, download_url, page_url` docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:91:- Animated: subtle pulse glow on download count numbers docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:100:│ CoX MOD TERMINAL [🔍 search mods...] │ docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:119:- Category tab click → live filter (no reload) docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:120:- Search input → filters name + description (debounced 200ms) docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:121:- Sort dropdown → downloads desc / name A-Z / author A-Z docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:122:- Download button → `window.open(mod.download_url)` (opens cityofheroes.dev download) docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:135: scrape-mods.py ← consolidated scraper/installer docs/plans/2026-07-13-mod-browser-design.md:145:- Filter + search works live pages/v3/tools.html:6: Gadgets - Noir pages/v3/tools.html:15: pages/v3/tools.html:16:

docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:7:The ecosystem functions as both a personal toolkit and a community resource. On one side, it provides automated scripts and documentation to help players install mods—custom enhancements ranging from map overlays showing badge locations to sound replacements for power effects. On the other side, it preserves knowledge about managing Wine environments on modern macOS hardware, helping fellow CoH enthusiasts keep this classic alive. The architecture spans multiple tools: LaunchCat (the Wine wrapper), the official Mod Installer (which converts community downloads into native `.pigg` format), and the game itself, which loads these mods as first-class assets rather than loose file overrides. docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:13:**Personal Utility**: Instead of manually downloading dozens of mods from cityofheroes.dev, organizing them, and figuring out the correct asset paths, players can run a script or use the provided GUI tools. This saves hours of technical troubleshooting—time better spent flying through Paragon City. docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:17:**Community Contribution**: Through scripts like `download-mods.sh` and `README.md` guides, players contribute back to the small but dedicated community. The project already catalogs 291 mods across 8 categories—from the essential Vidiotmaps pack (31,000+ downloads) to niche audio replacements and cursor themes. docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:23:The mod installation journey follows a clear path from discovery to gameplay: docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:25:1. **Mod Discovery** — Scripts read mod listings from cityofheroes.dev/mods/ and prepare download URLs. The Mod Installer (GUI tool by Michiyo) provides an alternative entry point with one-click browsing. docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:33:5. **Game Launch** — LaunchCat loads the modded client through Wine 9.0 on Intel macOS. The game presents installed mods highlighted in red in the UI, with an "Update" button available for each. docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:35:The ecosystem architecture separates concerns cleanly: base game assets in `issue24/` (4.2 GB) remain untouched, live server updates in `live/` (584 MB) are managed by Homecoming, and community creations live in `mods/` (currently 32 MB from three installed packages). This separation ensures updates don't break custom content, while the `.pigg` format ensures performance doesn't suffer from customization. docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:92:| **Vidiotmaps** | AboveTheChemist | Map overlay mod (31k+ downloads) | docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:102:- ✅ **Game assets installed** (4.8 GB total across issue24/live) docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:104:- ✅ **Three mods installed** (BetterIcons, accolade_lrt, PocketDAssemblage23) docs/coh-ecosystem-narrative.md:105:- ⏳ **291 mods catalogued** in `coh-mods-list.txt` (ready for batch install) Upgraded/2026-07-13-155340-usersstevenclaudeprojects-users-steven-cox.txt:40: - Downloaders in both Python (download-mods.py) and Bash (download-mods.sh) Upgraded/2026-07-13-155340-usersstevenclaudeprojects-users-steven-cox.txt:46: 1.
